parsed_review.summary: CP00-826 (Platform Extensibility "Synthetic Fixture Set"->P03 interface bridge, Risk C, 150 units, RP27.P02.M06.S01-RP27.P03.M04.S01) is a descriptor-only closeout pack whose claims are supported by source inspected via Read/Grep/Glob. The manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status:in_progress, implementation_layer:"descriptor", descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false, so there is no production/runtime/enterprise-trust overclaim at this snapshot. registry.js defines PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP826_PACK_BINDING (CP00-826, Risk C, 150 units, S01-S01, upstream CP00-825, next CP00-827/RP27.P03.M04.S02) and CP826 requirements whose deliverable_counts {contract:18,implementation:63,security_audit:16,ui:15,claude_review:6,failure_recovery:10,test:21,hermes_evidence:1} and micro_title_row_counts reproduce the prompt distributions exactly and sum to 150 (P02=108, P03=42). createPlatformExtensibilityCp826SyntheticFixtureInterfaceBridgeDescriptor (source_descriptor CP825) spreads the all-false PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and sets authority_boundary {claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true}; the Hermes packet sets emits_runtime_receipt:false and the Claude packet sets read_only:true / promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false; closeout_handoff routes to CP00-827/RP27.P03.M04.S02. createRow bakes descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, and no-secret/no-real-data flags into every row, with per-row-type ROW_EXTRAS reinforcing no-write/no-leak boundaries. validators.js enforces coverage (plan/risk/unit/first/last/dedup/RP27-only/deliverable/phase/micro/title/section rows), descriptor (type, source, program id, authority boundary, per-row descriptor_only + runtime_execution:false + no API-key/webhook/workflow secrets + no real client data/permission detail/audit body, no runtime opening via api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed, no permission/audit/product-state writes, Hermes/Claude packet, handoff, model registry), and contract (schema, RP27, latest_pack CP00-826, latest_projection descriptor, validation.valid, mandatory artifacts, no-leak guards). validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs imports from packages/platform/src/index.js (export * re-exports all CP826 symbols), checks the CP826->CP825 upstream binding, and runs coverage+descriptor+contract+capability+gate validation, exiting 1 on any error. model.test.js adds three CP826 tests asserting empty coverage/descriptor errors, deliverable counts, phase counts 108/42, api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed=false, source descriptor, handoff, and contract currency. The committed contracts/platform-extensibility-contract.json has current_pack/latest_pack CP00-826, latest_projection PlatformExtensibilityCp826SyntheticFixtureInterfaceBridgeDescriptor, projections.cp826, historical_packs CP820-825, no_leak_guards, and validation.valid:true with an all-false no_write_attestation. The live closeout-pack-plan.json lists CP00-826 as the first/live pack with within_default_risk_range:true and source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65..., and next-pack-queue.json shows the same ledger sha, live_latest_pack_id CP00-825, and live_next_unit_id RP27.P02.M06.S01, confirming CP00-826 is the live next pack with no plan drift. No API-key/secret/password/private-key/real-data patterns were found in packages/platform/src. Review hardening checks pass: auth preflight exit 0 with a 1228-byte well-formed JSON result (non-zero, not malformed, not fenced/prose, not tool-call-shaped), forbidden_tools_present:false, review schema present, summary_only review correctly disallowed (Risk C + security/audit + review-evidence deliverables) and not used, source_inspection_mode read_tools_used, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty, prompt 2726 bytes within the 25600 limit, tools Read/Grep/Glob only. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling and descriptor-only evidence packets, not product runtime. Three non-blocking P3 boundary notes: (1) downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present and, as a read-only reviewer without Bash, I could not independently execute the 10 validation commands, so this PASS is conditioned on the deterministic closeout producing passing command-evidence and on human final approval before any runtime/enterprise-trust opening; (2) the production_ready_flag string label could be misread as an assertion while production_ready is false; (3) changed-file-scope.json classifies docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md as both an untracked implementation file and an unrelated preserved file. blocks_goal_closeout is false because the pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust (runtime_ready:false; enterprise trust closed); that authority boundary is acknowledged as external to this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts pending; 10 validation commands not independently executable by read-only reviewer
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-826/ currently contains only manifest.json (the closed pack CP00-825 has all five). This is the expected pre-closeout state: this Claude review precedes adjudication/inspection and produces claude-review-result.json downstream; pack_level_claude_review.status is pending_review with empty valid_review_receipts and no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json yet in artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-826/. Because the review is read-only with no Bash, I could not independently execute the 10 expected validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check); I verified the binding, validator logic, tests, live plan/queue, ledger sha, and the contract projection by inspection only. The manifest still shows status:in_progress, production_ready:false, and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false, so no claim is overstated at this point.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready and committing, ensure the deterministic closeout pipeline produces command-evidence.json capturing passing results for all 10 validation commands, plus adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json, and that human final approval is recorded prior to any runtime/enterprise-trust opening. Treat this PASS as conditioned on that command-evidence.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Expected pre-closeout state confirmed against the closed CP00-825 pattern; does not block pack or goal closeout. Surfaced to bound the scope of a read-only review.

findings.title: production_ready_flag label may read as an assertion while production_ready is false
findings.details: production_ready_flag is set to "platform_extensibility_synthetic_fixture_interface_bridge_descriptor_verified" while production_ready:false (line 6) and implementation_summary.production_ready_descriptor_verified:false (line 1561). The adjacent _descriptor_verified:false and top-level production_ready:false make the true state unambiguous, and the *_flag string is a stable per-pack identifier (consistent with the CP820-CP826 bindings in registry.js and the contract current_pack/latest_pack), not an assertion of readiness. A future reader skimming only the flag name could nonetheless misinterpret it as a verified claim.
findings.recommendation: Optionally clarify (naming or a comment) that production_ready_flag is a descriptor label, not a readiness assertion, and that production_ready / production_ready_descriptor_verified are authoritative. No code or data change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking readability nit. State is internally disambiguated by production_ready:false and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists new-session-handoff.md as both untracked implementation file and unrelated preserved file
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 144) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 151), which are semantically opposed categories. This is immaterial to the active pack boundary: the active scope is defined by active_pack_implementation_refs (the 5 modified RP27 files: contracts/platform-extensibility-contract.json, packages/platform/src/registry.js, packages/platform/src/validators.js, packages/platform/test/model.test.js, scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs) plus active_pack_required_untracked_files (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-826), neither of which includes new-session-handoff.md, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required active file is dropped. scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs and tools/progress-control-room/index.html are correctly kept out of the active refs and listed as unrelated dirty files.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the changed-file-scope generator so a preserved/unrelated file is not simultaneously emitted under untracked_implementation_files, to keep provenance auditing unambiguous. No impact on the pack deliverables.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking provenance bookkeeping note in the review tooling artifact (not a pack deliverable). Active pack scope remains correctly bounded.
